title: MAP 6.13: Add trace schema, calibration, and robustness grading plan
description: Wave 2 from MAP 6.13 plan. Define traceable visibility, event append/reconstruction behavior, real-parameter measurement, sensitivity/robustness grading, and two simulation-test-drive acceptance probes. Read MAP_System/artifacts/planning/map-613-master-implementation-plan.md, MAP_System/events/README.md, MAP_System/scripts/validate_events.py, MAP_System/notes/communication-architecture.md, and Guidelines/6.13/files8/MAP-Simulation-Results-Round6.md before editing.
acceptance_criteria: Trace schema defines trace_id, parent_event_id, actor, action, target, task_id, and thread while preserving legacy event compatibility.
Calibration plan measures shipped-defect vs false-halt cost, local-vs-cloud defect rate, file churn, compression ratio, latency, hcom volume, and operator approval load.
Robustness method classifies conclusions as robust, conditional, or unsupported across plausible parameter ranges.
Probe artifact defines acceptance tests for shared-state coordination and validator halt authority.
